content.introduction: Insertion sort builds a sorted prefix by inserting each new element into its proper position.

sections.title: 7.4.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define sorted-prefix invariant and insertion-by-shift mechanism for each pass.
examples: Explain why insertion sort is stable
Identify sorted prefix after each pass

sections.title: 7.4.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe pass-by-pass process, inner-loop shifting, and stable ordering behavior.
examples: Count shifts for nearly sorted input
Implement in-place insertion with key variable

sections.title: 7.4.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(n^2) worst/average, O(n) best on nearly sorted data, with O(1) extra space.
examples: Choose insertion sort for small arrays
Use it as base case in hybrid sorts
Compare performance on nearly sorted datasets

keyTakeaways: Use insertion sort in small or nearly sorted cases
